description: In an apartment, Rachel stands wearing a black jacket and overalls, holding a black garment. She gestures with both hands and says, "That is not true. So you just sort of happened to leave it in here?"
Monica, standing opposite Rachel, opens her arms wide and replies, "Did it ever occur to you that I might just be that stupid?" Rachel stands with her hands down.
Rachel places her right hand over her chest and breathes in.
Rachel, keeping her hand on her chest, says, "Okay, Monica, I just have to know one thing."
Monica stands still. Rachel lowers her head slightly, closing her eyes briefly, then opens them and looks at Monica.
Rachel asks, "Did you go with her to Bloomingdale?"
Monica's eyes widen, and she quickly raises both hands to cover her mouth, making an audible gasp.
Rachel's mouth drops open. Monica keeps her hands over her mouth.
Rachel raises her right hand and says, "Um."
Rachel lowers her hand, turns away, and begins walking toward a doorway. Monica remains standing near the couch.
Rachel says, "Okay."
Rachel touches her hair with her right hand, then steps forward again, looking down.
Holding her right hand up, Rachel says, "I just really I just really need to not be with you right now." Monica stands with her fingers intertwined in front of her.
Monica stands with her fingers intertwined. The setting changes to an exterior view of a brick apartment building.
A yellow taxi drives past the apartment building.
The setting changes to an infant lying in a crib with toys. Chandler and Ross rest their heads on the edges of the crib.
In the background, Monica holds a phone to her ear and says, "Hi."
Monica shifts slightly in her chair while holding the phone.
Monica sits in the chair and says, "Who's this? Hi, Joanne. Is Rachel working? It's Monica."
Monica holds the phone to her ear, listening.
Monica gestures with her free hand and says, "Yes, I know I did a horrible thing."
The setting changes to the main living area, where Phoebe stands beside Joey, who is seated at a table looking at a book.
Monica walks past them holding the phone and says, "Joanne, it's not as simple as all that, okay?"
Monica stops walking and looks upward while listening.
Monica throws her free hand up and says, "No, I don't care what Steve thinks. Hi, Steve." She continues walking.
Monica walks out of the room. Phoebe walks to the front door, opens it, and two women enter.
Joey turns around as the women say, "Hey."
The two women smile as Phoebe closes the door behind them.
Phoebe asks, "How did we do?" Carol says, "Oh, I tasted Ben's milk and Ross freaked out." Ross, seated by the crib, raises his hands and says, "I did not freak out."
Carol asks, "Why'd you freak"
Carol finishes her sentence, "out?" Ross gestures and says, "Because it's breast milk."
Ross lowers his hands and looks at Carol.
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "It's gross."
Susan, sitting next to Carol, asks, "My breast milk is gross?"
Ross looks toward Susan, his mouth slightly open.
Ross raises his hand and says, "This should be"
The women sit on the couch while Chandler and Joey stand nearby, all looking at Ross.
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "no, no, Carol, there's nothing wrong with it. I just don't think breast milk is for adults."
Ross looks down slightly and adds, "Of course, the"
Looking toward the women, Ross finishes his sentence, "packaging does appeal to grown ups and kids."
Carol leans forward on the back of the couch.
Carol holds up a baby bottle toward Ross and says, "Ross, you are being silly. I've tried it. It's no big deal. Come on, just taste it."
Ross stands up and gestures with his hands.
Ross steps back and says, "That would be no."
Carol turns around toward Chandler, who smiles and nods slightly. Phoebe and Joey watch from nearby.
Joey gestures with his hand and says, "it doesn't taste bad. Yeah, it tastes kind of sweet."
Ross looks at Joey and asks, "Sort of like what?" Susan asks, "Cantaloupe juice?" Joey points at her and says, "Exactly."
Ross looks at Joey, his expression still. Chandler and the women watch.
Ross points a finger toward Joey, raising his eyebrows.
Ross, pointing his finger at Joey, says, "You've tasted it."
Joey looks at Ross without speaking.
Ross nods slightly and says, "You've tasted it."
Susan looks at Ross and smiles.
Ross nods again and says, "Oh, you've tasted it."
Joey stands with his hands in his pockets, looking at Ross. Susan points at Joey.
Susan gestures with her hand and says, "You can keep saying it, but it won't stop being true."
Ross turns away from Joey and looks toward the couch, smiling slightly.
Ross steps toward the crib, rubbing his hands together.
Ross holds out his hand and says, "Get me the bottle."
Joey points to the bottle in Carol's hand, then takes it and hands it to Ross.
Ross takes the bottle, looks at it, and extends his other arm.
Ross holds his arm out and says, "Get me the towel."
Joey picks up a white towel and places it over Ross's extended forearm.
Susan smiles as the group watches Ross hold the bottle and towel.
The setting changes to an exterior view of a building at night. A restaurant with red window frames is visible on the corner.
Inside an apartment, Chandler wipes the kitchen counter with a cloth. The door opens and Joey enters.
Joey is wearing a shiny white western-style shirt, white pants, and a white cowboy hat. Chandler stops wiping and stares at him.
Joey closes the door and walks toward the kitchen counter. Chandler places his hands on his hips, watching Joey approach.
Chandler looks at Joey and says, "Howdy?"
Joey looks at Chandler without responding.
In a kitchen, Chandler stands at the counter wiping it with a cloth. Joey sits across from him wearing a white cowboy hat.
Joey says, "Give me a box of juice." Chandler stops wiping and walks toward the refrigerator.
Chandler opens the refrigerator, pulls out a small juice box, and closes the door. He returns to the counter.

Chandler sets the juice box in front of Joey. Joey looks up and says, "Well, they switched me over to hombre." Chandler holds another juice box and a straw, replying, "Well, maybe it's because of the way you're dressed." Joey takes off his hat. Audience laughter plays.
Joey places his hat on the counter. Chandler unwraps the straw for his juice box.
Joey gestures with both hands, saying, "Or maybe it's because this guy's doing so good they want to put more people on it. You should see this guy," Chandler pours juice from a bottle into a glass.
Joey points toward Chandler and continues, "Chandler. He goes through, like, two bottles a day now." Chandler finishes pouring and sets the bottle down.
Chandler places his hands on the counter edge and leans forward.
Chandler asks, "What do you care?" while looking steadily at Joey.
Joey looks back at Chandler.
Chandler gestures with the cloth in his right hand, saying, "You're an actor. This is your day job. This isn't supposed to mean anything to you."
Joey replies, "I know, but I was the best. You".
Joey looks down, gesturing with one hand, and adds, "know? I liked being the best."
Joey lowers his head and wipes his mouth with his hand. Chandler picks up the juice bottle and pours more into his glass. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey taps the counter and gestures outward, saying, "I don't know, maybe I should just get out of the game. They need guys up in housewares to serve cheese." Chandler holds the bottle.
Chandler sets the bottle down again.
Chandler holds the cloth in both hands and says, "All right, say you do that. Sooner or later, somebody's going to come along that slices of better cheddar."
Chandler looks at Joey without speaking.
Chandler tosses the cloth over his left shoulder and asks, "And then where are you going to run?"

Joey looks down and says softly, "Yeah, I guess you're right." Chandler leans in, points his right index finger at Joey, and says louder, "You're damn right I'm right. I say you show this guy what you're made of. I say you"
Chandler clenches his right fist, raising his volume further, and continues, "stand your ground. I say you show him that you are the baddest hombre west of the lingerie." Audience laughter plays.
Joey sits up straighter, keeping his gaze on Chandler.
Joey points at Chandler and says firmly, "I'm gonna do it." Chandler replies, "All right,"
Joey picks up his glass and drinks. Chandler grabs the cloth from his shoulder.
Chandler wipes his hands with the cloth and says, "now, go see Miss Kitty and she'll fix you up with a nice hooker." Joey lowers his glass and walks past Chandler. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler continues wiping his hands. The setting changes to a city street at night, outside a storefront window with a sign for Central Perk.

Monica, wearing a black sleeveless dress, stands near Rachel, who wears a white t-shirt and white apron. Rachel wipes an outdoor table. Monica says, "I don't know what else to say." Rachel replies, "Oh, well, that works out good, because I'm not listening."
Monica turns her head slightly, keeping her gaze on Rachel.
Monica says, "I feel terrible."

Monica continues, "I really do." Rachel picks up a rag, turns her back to Monica, and walks toward the door, saying over her shoulder, "Oh, I'm sorry. Did my back hurt your knife?" Audience laughter plays.
Now inside the coffee shop, Rachel walks past a counter carrying a tray. Monica follows close behind. Phoebe sits on a sofa nearby reading a magazine.
Rachel sets the tray down and turns around. Monica asks, "Say that I'm friends with her. We spend some time together. Is that so terrible?"
Rachel replies loudly, "Yes, it's that terrible. Yes. Monica, you don't"
Rachel presses both hands to her upper chest and continues, her voice trembling slightly, "get it. It's bad enough that she stole the guy who might actually be the person that I am supposed to be"
Rachel keeps her hands on her chest, looking at Monica.
Rachel adds, "actually."
Rachel moves one hand to her mouth.
Rachel's voice breaks as she says, "But now she's actually stealing you"
Rachel covers her face with both hands, visibly crying. Phoebe looks up from her magazine toward them. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica places a hand on her own chest, then reaches toward Rachel, saying, "what are you talking about? Nobody could steal me from you." Rachel lowers her hands and wipes her forehead.
Monica keeps her hands near her waist.
Monica gestures with her hands and says, "I mean, just because I'm friends with her doesn't make me any less friends with you."
Rachel looks at Monica, her eyes narrowed and her face tense.
Rachel presses a hand to her chest, her voice choked, and says, "You might."
Rachel covers her face again with her hands.
Rachel lowers her hands slightly and says, "Weird."
Rachel covers her face with both hands, crying. Monica looks at her, her own face drawn. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica raises her hands open, stepping closer, and says, "Oh, I love you."
Rachel steps forward and wraps her arms around Monica. They hold each other in a tight hug. The audience makes a sustained vocalization.
Phoebe stands up, walks over to the hugging pair, and says, "You guys, I know that this really doesn't have anything to do with me but I love you guys too." Audience laughter plays.
Phoebe wraps her arms around both Rachel and Monica, joining the embrace.
The three women hold the group hug as audience laughter continues.
Phoebe steps backward away from the hug, saying, "I really needed that."
Rachel and Monica separate. Rachel wipes her nose and eyes with her hand.
Monica looks at Rachel and says softly, "Look, I know that you're in a place right now where you really need to hate Julie's guts."
Monica reaches out to gently touch Rachel's forehead and hair, adding, "But she didn't do anything wrong." Rachel keeps her gaze lowered.
Monica gestures toward her own waist as she says, "I mean, she's just a girl who met a guy and now they go out."
Rachel looks up at Monica.
Monica leans forward slightly, saying, "And I really think that if you gave her a chance, you'd like her."
Rachel looks down again, her expression subdued.
Monica touches her own hair, then asks, "Will you just give that a chance?"
Monica reaches out and touches Rachel's arm.
Monica asks softly, "For me?"
Rachel looks at Monica, her lips pressed in a tight line.
Rachel gestures slightly with one hand and says, "I do anything for you. You know that."
Monica steps forward and wraps her arms around Rachel, hugging her again.
Phoebe rushes back toward them, arms outstretched, and reenters the group hug, shouting, "Waiting." Audience laughter plays.
The view changes to a daytime exterior shot of a dense city skyline with multiple tall buildings.
The daytime view of the city skyline remains visible.
In a department store, Joey, wearing an all-white cowboy outfit and a white hat, walks past racks of clothing.
Joey stops near Hombre Man, who is wearing an all-black cowboy outfit and a black hat and leaning against a doorway structure.
Hombre Man says, "Morning," without subtitles. Joey looks away and exhales audibly.
"I said, morning," Hombre Man says, adjusting his position.
Hombre Man smiles slightly as Joey turns his head back toward him.
Joey adjusts his white neckerchief and says, "I heard you."
A man in a suit walks briskly past them toward a set of double glass doors.
The man in the suit turns back to the cowboys and says, "All right, everybody, I'm opening the doors."
The man in the suit grips the door handles.
"You boys ready?" the man in the suit asks.
Hombre Man takes a drag from a cigarette, exhales, and tosses it away.
Joey nods slightly.
"Yeah, I'm ready," Joey says.
Western-style music starts playing. Joey and Hombre Man step apart and face each other across the aisle, assuming wide-legged stances.
Joey and Hombre Man keep their hands hovering near their belts. Hombre Man narrows his eyes, and Joey narrows his eyes in response.
The man in the suit pulls the double glass doors open. Joey and Hombre Man maintain their stances and stare at each other.
Joey's right hand flexes near a black cologne bottle holstered on his belt. The man in the suit opens the doors fully.
An older man in a blue short-sleeve shirt walks through the doors. Joey and Hombre Man both turn their heads toward him.
Joey stares intently at the older man. The older man smiles and continues walking. Hombre Man also stares at the older man.
Joey's eyes widen. He holds his stance as the older man walks further into the store.
Joey quickly draws his cologne bottle and strides toward the older man. Hombre Man simultaneously draws his own bottle and steps forward.

Hombre Man steps directly in front of the older man and sprays cologne into his face. The Western-style music stops as the older man covers his eyes with his hands and shouts. Audience laughter plays.
The older man bends his knees, continuing to shout and cover his eyes. Joey lowers his bottle and steps back slightly.
The older man yells, "You idiot. You stupid cowboy," while keeping his hands over his eyes. Hombre Man points at him.
"You blinded me," the older man shouts.
The older man yells, "I'm suing. Oh, my God," as the man in the suit grabs his arm and turns him around.
"Oh, my God," the older man continues, supported by the man in the suit.
"Todd, what the hell did you do?" the man in the suit shouts at Hombre Man. Joey steps forward, places a hand on his head, and says, "I'm sorry. I'm such a doofus. I'm so sorry."
"I'm so sorry," Joey continues, patting Hombre Man on the back as Hombre Man is ushered away.
Joey turns back around to face the aisle.
A blonde woman in a black dress approaches Joey and asks, "My God. What happened?"
Joey turns his attention to the blonde woman.
"These new kids, they never last," Joey says to her.
Joey pauses and looks toward the doors.
"Sooner or later, they all" Joey continues, raising his right hand to point.
Joey pauses briefly.
"stop lasting," Joey finishes, dropping his hand.
The blonde woman nods and crosses her arms.
"Listen, what do you say I buy you that cup of coffee now?" Joey asks, adjusting his belt.
The blonde woman smiles.
"Sure," the blonde woman says.
Joey and the blonde woman link arms and walk away down the hallway.
They walk toward a painted sunset mural at the end of the hall. The scene changes to the interior of a coffee shop, where people sit at tables.
Rachel and Julie sit side by side on a sofa. Rachel looks down and adjusts a magazine on the table in front of them.
"It so" Rachel says, keeping her gaze downward.
Rachel looks at her lap, then turns her head slightly toward Julie.
Rachel crosses her arms, shifts her weight, and returns her gaze forward.
Rachel turns her head toward Julie and says, "I just thought the two of us should hang out for a bit. I mean, you know, we've never really talked."
Rachel pauses and looks down briefly.
"I guess, you'd know that, being one of the two of us. All right, I know" Rachel continues, gesturing with one hand before crossing her arms and smiling. Audience laughter plays.
Rachel rubs her neck with her hand.
Julie gestures with her hands and says, "I probably shouldn't even tell you this"
"but I'm pretty much totally intimidated by you," Julie adds, her hands still moving.
Rachel looks at Julie, her eyes widening slightly.
"Really?" Rachel asks.
Julie nods slightly.
"Oh, my God, are you kidding? Ross is so crazy about you," Julie says, gesturing.
"and I really wanted you to like me," Julie continues. Rachel uncrosses her arms.
"And it's probably me just being totally paranoid" Julie says, bringing her hands to her lap.
"but I kind of got the feeling that maybe you" Julie continues. Rachel looks down, then back at her.
"don't," Julie finishes her sentence.
Rachel looks at Julie with a still expression, her arms crossed again.
Rachel looks down, rubs her neck, and exhales softly.
"Well," Rachel says, looking at Julie.
Rachel pauses, maintaining eye contact.
"you're not totally paranoid," Rachel says, smiling slightly.
Julie's smile fades as she looks at Rachel.
In a coffee shop, Rachel and Julie sit on an orange couch. Julie rests her elbow on a cushion and smiles at Rachel.
An uncertain speaker says, "Okay?"
Rachel looks down, her eyes lowered.
Rachel exhales and says, "God," her gaze remaining lowered.
Rachel keeps her head down, while Julie continues to look at her.
Rachel looks up slightly and says, "when you and Ross first started going out it was really hard for me," pausing briefly.
Rachel shakes her head slightly, looking down again.
Rachel looks toward Julie, saying, "for many reasons of which I'm not going to bore you with now. But" while gesturing slightly with her hand.
Rachel looks away again. Julie maintains her gaze on Rachel with a neutral expression.
Rachel gestures with her right hand and says, "I see how happy he is now and how good you guys are together."
Rachel tilts her head.
Rachel continues, "And" while shifting her posture.
Rachel maintains eye contact with Julie.
Rachel says, "Monica's always saying how nice you are and God, I hate it when she's right." Julie smiles broadly. Audience laughter begins.
Julie laughs and looks down, then back at Rachel. Audience laughter continues.
"Thanks," Julie says, smiling.
Julie gestures with her hands and asks, "Hey, listen, would you like to go to a movie sometime or something?"
Rachel nods and replies, "Yeah, that'd be great."
"Yeah, I'd love it," Julie says, smiling. Rachel adds, "I'd love it"
Rachel finishes, "too." Julie says, "Okay."
Julie shifts her posture on the couch.
Julie looks at her watch and says, "Oh, shoot. I got to go," beginning to stand up. Rachel says, "Okay, I'll talk to you later." Julie says, "Okay." Rachel adds, "All right, Julie."
Julie stands fully and says, "Bye."
Julie turns and walks away. Rachel leans back, crosses her arms, and watches Julie leave.
Rachel turns her head forward, resting it against the back of the couch, her arms remaining crossed.
Rachel mutters, "But a manipulative bitch," her gaze directed straight ahead. Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel sits motionless with her arms crossed. An uncertain speaker says, "it's." Audience laughter continues.
The setting changes to an apartment kitchen. Upbeat instrumental music begins playing. A baby bottle and a plate of cookies rest on a wooden table. Ross sits behind the table, rubbing his chest.
Ross looks down at the table. Beside him, Chandler rests his chin on his hand.
Across the table, Joey rests his head on his hand. Chandler stretches his arms forward, lowering his head onto the table.
Ross touches his head, then reaches forward toward the baby bottle.
Ross picks up the bottle, brings the nipple to his mouth, and drinks from it.
Ross continues drinking from the bottle. Chandler lifts his head, looks at Ross, and smiles.
Ross stops drinking and pulls the bottle away from his mouth, his eyes widening.
Ross lowers the bottle and speaks, gesturing with one hand. Joey responds, gesturing with his hands.
Joey points at the bottle and speaks. Ross replies, pointing at the bottle as well.
Joey reaches for the bottle. The scene darkens to black.
The screen remains black as the upbeat instrumental music continues.
The instrumental music fades into silence.
